title: Validate observed data in Prometheus and Loki
releaseGate: Owner upgraded scope after batch-012: metrics must be queryable in Prometheus; server local logs, server syslog logs, network syslog logs, and network SNMP trap logs must be queryable in Loki or recorded as exact blockers.

stories.title: Prepare Prometheus and Loki query tooling
stories.scope: Add reusable Prometheus/Loki query APIs to oneops_test_tool so observed-data validation can use stable local endpoints instead of ad hoc curl commands.
nonGoals: Do not change OneOPS onboarding behavior in this story.
Do not assert data-plane success just because query tooling works.
Do not store credentials or large raw logs in evidence.
acceptance: oneops_test_tool exposes Prometheus instant/range/metrics query APIs.
oneops_test_tool exposes Loki labels/range query APIs.
Responses include backend endpoint, upstream status, result type, hit count, and stream count where applicable.
Live checks against Prometheus and Loki endpoints are recorded.

stories.title: Define observed-data evidence contract
stories.scope: Define the observed-data onboarding evidence keys and query catalog for Prometheus metrics plus Loki server local logs, server syslog, network syslog, and SNMP trap. The contract must distinguish success, pending/no_samples, blocked, and query_failed.
nonGoals: Do not claim observed-data success without a query hit.
Do not introduce a new onboarding DAG.
Do not persist full raw log payloads.
acceptance: Evidence keys are named explicitly: metric_observed, server_local_log_observed, server_syslog_observed, network_syslog_observed, snmp_trap_observed.
Each key records query backend, endpoint, query summary, window, hit_count, stream_count, sample summary, and blocker.
No-sample results are preserved as pending/no_samples, not success.
Focused tests or contract examples cover success and no-samples outcomes.
notes: Use D2ON-049 query-tool response shape as the evidence input contract.

stories.title: Validate Prometheus metrics for ready devices
stories.scope: Use oneops_test_tool Prometheus APIs to verify metric samples for selected ready devices. Prefer known ready network devices with ping/SNMP metrics and record exact queries and hit counts.
nonGoals: Do not run broad fleet validation.
Do not treat metric-name presence as device-specific success.
Do not change monitoring templates unless a precise blocker requires it.
acceptance: At least one selected ready device has a Prometheus query result or exact no-samples/blocker evidence.
Evidence records PromQL, endpoint, window, hit_count, and selected device identity.
If labels do not map to device_code, the label mismatch is recorded explicitly.
notes: Prometheus metric names currently include ping_result_code and snmp_interface_ifOperStatus.

stories.title: Validate server local and server syslog logs in Loki
stories.scope: Use oneops_test_tool Loki range queries to validate server local log and server syslog samples for a bounded server device. Record hits or exact blockers separately for local-file/tail and syslog paths.
nonGoals: Do not claim server local logs from server syslog samples.
Do not dump large raw log bodies into evidence.
Do not require alert generation.
acceptance: server_local_log_observed and server_syslog_observed are recorded separately.
Each Loki query records endpoint, LogQL, window, hit_count, stream_count, and sample summary or exact no-samples blocker.
If the collector has no samples, evidence points to the listener/task/config stage that should be repaired next.
notes: Known server syslog delivery device from prior scope: DVC8B7374049C21.

D2ON-052 closed with server_syslog_observed=queryable and server_local_log_observed=blocked: tail task is converged/running with grok and name_override=tail, but no Loki tail series appears and remote agent file upload/download is not implemented for a safe local probe line.

stories.title: Validate network syslog logs in Loki
stories.scope: Use oneops_test_tool Loki range queries to validate network-device syslog samples for a bounded ready H3C/Comware device. Record hits or exact blockers without reopening the configuration/readback closure.
nonGoals: Do not rerun broad batch onboarding.
Do not assume syslog target configured means Loki data arrived.
Do not validate every network vendor.
acceptance: network_syslog_observed records the Loki query result for a selected ready network device.
Evidence includes device code, IP, listener endpoint, LogQL, window, hit_count, stream_count, and sample summary or exact blocker.
No-samples outcome is not reported as success.
notes: D2ON-053 closed with network_syslog_observed=no_samples: target configuration/readback is successful and Loki has server syslog samples, but no source/hostname/message match for 172.32.2.14 / R11 appears in the bounded query window.

stories.title: Validate network SNMP trap logs in Loki
stories.scope: Use oneops_test_tool Loki range queries to validate SNMP trap samples from the H3C/Comware trap target path. If no trap is naturally emitted, record exact no-samples evidence and the next bounded generation strategy.
nonGoals: Do not claim trap data-plane closure from target readback alone.
Do not send unsafe disruptive trap-generating commands.
Do not require alert/RCA validation.
acceptance: snmp_trap_observed records a Loki query result for trap samples or exact no-samples blocker.
Evidence includes managed trap listener identity, endpoint, LogQL, window, hit_count, stream_count, and sample summary if present.
If a synthetic or device-triggered trap is needed, the strategy is documented before execution.
notes: D2ON-048 configured trap target for DVCD25E1C13D3C3 to 192.168.100.6:162.

D2ON-054 closed with snmp_trap_observed=no_samples: trap target configuration/readback is successful, but no Loki snmp_trap stream or trap/SNMP syslog fallback sample appears in the bounded 6h query window.

stories.title: Close observed-data readiness
stories.scope: Summarize observed-data closure for batch-013. Mark each category as queryable, pending/no_samples, blocked, or failed, and update readiness/review without reopening batch-012.
nonGoals: Do not hide partial data-plane results behind one aggregate success.
Do not claim alert/RCA closure.
Do not broaden to every device.
acceptance: Final readiness lists metrics, server local logs, server syslog, network syslog, and SNMP trap outcomes separately.
Each outcome links to machine-checkable evidence.
Open blockers are exact and actionable.
notes: This is the closure gate for the upgraded scope, not a code-only story.

D2ON-055 closes batch-013 as observed-data readiness with exact blockers: metrics and server syslog are queryable; server local tail is blocked; network syslog and SNMP trap are no_samples after successful device-side readback.
